briefdescription.para: Push a value on the stack.
Pop a value from the stack.
Raises an error.
Raises an XPATH_INVALID_ARITY error.
Raises an XPATH_INVALID_TYPE error.
Get the error code of an XPath context.

simplesect.para: the context error.

briefdescription.para: Check if an XPath error was raised.

simplesect.para: true if an error has been raised, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the document of an XPath context.

simplesect.para: the context document.

briefdescription.para: Get the context node of an XPath context.

simplesect.para: the context node.

briefdescription.para: Pushes the boolean val on the context stack.
Pushes true on the context stack.
Pushes false on the context stack.
Pushes the double val on the context stack.
Pushes the string str on the context stack.
Pushes an empty string on the stack.
Pushes the node-set ns on the context stack.
Pushes an empty node-set on the context stack.
Pushes user data on the context stack.
Check if the current value on the XPath stack is a node set or an XSLT value tree.

simplesect.para: true if the current object on the stack is a node-set.

briefdescription.para: Checks if the current value on the XPath stack is an external object.

simplesect.para: true if the current object on the stack is an external object.

briefdescription.para: Empties a node-set.
Macro to return from the function if an XPath error was detected.
Macro to return 0 from the function if an XPath error was detected.
Macro to raise an XPath error and return.
Macro to raise an XPath error and return 0.
Macro to check that the value on top of the XPath stack is of a given type.
Macro to check that the value on top of the XPath stack is of a given type.

detaileddescription.para: Return(0) in case of failure

memberdef.name: CHECK_ARITY
memberdef.initializer: if (ctxt == NULL) return; \ if (nargs != (x)) \ XP_ERROR(XPATH_INVALID_ARITY); \ if (ctxt->valueNr < (x)) \ XP_ERROR(XPATH_STACK_ERROR);

briefdescription.para: Macro to check that the number of args passed to an XPath function matches.
Macro to try to cast the value on the top of the XPath stack to a string.
Macro to try to cast the value on the top of the XPath stack to a number.
Macro to try to cast the value on the top of the XPath stack to a boolean.
Pops a boolean from the stack, handling conversion if needed.

detaileddescription.para: Check error with xmlXPathCheckError.

simplesect.para: the boolean

briefdescription.para: Pops a number from the stack, handling conversion if needed.

detaileddescription.para: Check error with xmlXPathCheckError.

simplesect.para: the number

briefdescription.para: Pops a string from the stack, handling conversion if needed.

detaileddescription.para: Check error with xmlXPathCheckError.

simplesect.para: the string

briefdescription.para: Pops a node-set from the stack, handling conversion if needed.

detaileddescription.para: Check error with xmlXPathCheckError.

simplesect.para: the node-set

briefdescription.para: Pops an external object from the stack, handling conversion if needed.

detaileddescription.para: Check error with xmlXPathCheckError.

simplesect.para: the object

briefdescription.para: register an external mechanism to do variable lookup
Registers an external mechanism to do function lookup.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContext *ctxt, const char *file, int line, int no)
memberdef.name: xmlXPatherror

briefdescription.para: Formats an error message.
Handle an XPath error.
Dump the content of the object for debugging purposes.

parameterdescription.para: the FILE * to dump the output

briefdescription.para: Dumps the tree of the compiled XPath expression.
NodeSet handling.

detaileddescription.para: NodeSet handling.

simplesect.para: true (1) if cur contains val, false (0) otherwise

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets difference() function: node-set set:difference (node-set, node-set)

simplesect.para: the difference between the two node sets, or nodes1 if nodes2 is empty

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets intersection() function: node-set set:intersection (node-set, node-set)

simplesect.para: a node set comprising the nodes that are within both the node sets passed as arguments

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets distinct() function: node-set set:distinct (node-set)

parameterdescription.para: a node-set, sorted by document order

simplesect.para: a subset of the nodes contained in nodes, or nodes if it is empty

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets distinct() function: node-set set:distinct (node-set) nodes is sorted by document order, then exslSetsDistinctSorted is called with the sorted node-set.

simplesect.para: a subset of the nodes contained in nodes, or nodes if it is empty

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets has-same-nodes function: boolean set:has-same-node(node-set, node-set)

simplesect.para: true (1) if nodes1 shares any node with nodes2, false (0) otherwise

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets leading() function: node-set set:leading (node-set, node-set)

parameterdescription.para: a node-set, sorted by document order

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes that precede node in document order, nodes if node is NULL or an empty node-set if nodes doesn't contain node

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets leading() function: node-set set:leading (node-set, node-set)

parameterdescription.para: a node-set, sorted by document order
a node-set, sorted by document order

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes1 that precede the first node in nodes2 in document order, nodes1 if nodes2 is NULL or empty or an empty node-set if nodes1 doesn't contain nodes2

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets leading() function: node-set set:leading (node-set, node-set) nodes is sorted by document order, then exslSetsNodeLeadingSorted is called.

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes that precede node in document order, nodes if node is NULL or an empty node-set if nodes doesn't contain node

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets leading() function: node-set set:leading (node-set, node-set) nodes1 and nodes2 are sorted by document order, then exslSetsLeadingSorted is called.

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes1 that precede the first node in nodes2 in document order, nodes1 if nodes2 is NULL or empty or an empty node-set if nodes1 doesn't contain nodes2

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets trailing() function: node-set set:trailing (node-set, node-set)

parameterdescription.para: a node-set, sorted by document order

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes that follow node in document order, nodes if node is NULL or an empty node-set if nodes doesn't contain node

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets trailing() function: node-set set:trailing (node-set, node-set)

parameterdescription.para: a node-set, sorted by document order
a node-set, sorted by document order

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes1 that follow the first node in nodes2 in document order, nodes1 if nodes2 is NULL or empty or an empty node-set if nodes1 doesn't contain nodes2

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets trailing() function: node-set set:trailing (node-set, node-set) nodes is sorted by document order, then xmlXPathNodeTrailingSorted is called.

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes that follow node in document order, nodes if node is NULL or an empty node-set if nodes doesn't contain node

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets trailing() function: node-set set:trailing (node-set, node-set) nodes1 and nodes2 are sorted by document order, then xmlXPathTrailingSorted is called.

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes1 that follow the first node in nodes2 in document order, nodes1 if nodes2 is NULL or empty or an empty node-set if nodes1 doesn't contain nodes2

briefdescription.para: Extending a context.

detaileddescription.para: Extending a context.
If ns_uri is NULL it unregisters the namespace

parameterdescription.para: the namespace prefix cannot be NULL or empty string

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Search in the namespace declaration array of the context for the given namespace name associated to the given prefix.

simplesect.para: the value or NULL if not found

briefdescription.para: Cleanup the XPath context data associated to registered variables.
Register a new function.

detaileddescription.para: If f is NULL it unregisters the function

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathContext *ctxt, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *ns_uri, xmlXPathFunction f)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathRegisterFuncNS

briefdescription.para: Register a new function.

detaileddescription.para: If f is NULL it unregisters the function

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Register a new variable value.

detaileddescription.para: If value is NULL it unregisters the variable

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Register a new variable value.

detaileddescription.para: If value is NULL it unregisters the variable

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Search in the Function array of the context for the given function.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathFunction or NULL if not found

briefdescription.para: Search in the Function array of the context for the given function.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathFunction or NULL if not found

briefdescription.para: Cleanup the XPath context data associated to registered functions.
Search in the Variable array of the context for the given variable value.

simplesect.para: a copy of the value or NULL if not found

briefdescription.para: Search in the Variable array of the context for the given variable value.

simplesect.para: the a copy of the value or NULL if not found

briefdescription.para: Cleanup the XPath context data associated to registered variables.
Utilities to extend XPath.

detaileddescription.para: Utilities to extend XPath.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathParserContext just allocated.

briefdescription.para: Free up an xmlXPathParserContext.
Pops the top XPath object from the value stack.

simplesect.para: the XPath object just removed

briefdescription.para: Pushes a new XPath object on top of the value stack.

detaileddescription.para: If value is NULL, a memory error is recorded in the parser context.
The object is destroyed in case of error.

simplesect.para: the number of items on the value stack, or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type string and of value val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type string and of value val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Wraps the val string into an XPath object.

detaileddescription.para: Frees val in case of error.

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Wraps a string into an XPath object.

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type double and of value val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type boolean and of value val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type NodeSet and initialize it with the single Node val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type Value Tree (XSLT) and initialize it with the tree root val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: add a new xmlNode to an existing NodeSet

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: add a new xmlNode to an existing NodeSet, optimized version when we are sure the node is not already in the set.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of failure

briefdescription.para: add a new namespace node to an existing NodeSet

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Sort the node set in document order.
Initialize the context to the root of the document.
Parse and evaluate an XPath expression in the given context, then push the result on the context stack.
parse an XML name

simplesect.para: the name or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML non-colonized name.

simplesect.para: the nc name or NULL

briefdescription.para: [30a] Float ::= Number ('e' Digits?)?

detaileddescription.para: [30] Number ::= Digits ('.' Digits?)? | '.' Digits [31] Digits ::= [0-9]+
Compile a Number in the string In complement of the Number expression, this function also handles negative values : '-' Number.

simplesect.para: the double value.

briefdescription.para: Evaluate a predicate result for the current node.

detaileddescription.para: A PredicateExpr is evaluated by evaluating the Expr and converting the result to a boolean. If the result is a number, the result will be converted to true if the number is equal to the position of the context node in the context node list (as returned by the position function) and will be converted to false otherwise; if the result is not a number, then the result will be converted as if by a call to the boolean function.

simplesect.para: 1 if predicate is true, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Registers all default XPath functions in this context.
Merges two nodesets, all nodes from val2 are added to val1 if val1 is NULL, a new set is created and copied from val2

detaileddescription.para: Frees val1 in case of error.

simplesect.para: val1 once extended or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Removes an xmlNode from an existing NodeSet.
Removes an entry from an existing NodeSet list.
Create a new xmlXPathObject of type NodeSet and initialize it with the Nodeset val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Wrap the Nodeset val in a new xmlXPathObject.

detaileddescription.para: In case of error the node set is destroyed and NULL is returned.

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Wraps the val data into an XPath object.

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Implement the equal operation on XPath objects content: arg1 == arg2

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 depending on the results of the test.

briefdescription.para: Implement the equal operation on XPath objects content: arg1 == arg2

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 depending on the results of the test.

briefdescription.para: Implement the compare operation on XPath objects: arg1 < arg2 (1, 1, ... arg1 <= arg2 (1, 0, ... arg1 > arg2 (0, 1, ... arg1 >= arg2 (0, 0, ...

detaileddescription.para: When neither object to be compared is a node-set and the operator is <=, <, >=, >, then the objects are compared by converted both objects to numbers and comparing the numbers according to IEEE 754. The < comparison will be true if and only if the first number is less than the second number. The <= comparison will be true if and only if the first number is less than or equal to the second number. The > comparison will be true if and only if the first number is greater than the second number. The >= comparison will be true if and only if the first number is greater than or equal to the second number.

simplesect.para: 1 if the comparison succeeded, 0 if it failed

briefdescription.para: Implement the unary - operation on an XPath object The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.
Implement the add operation on XPath objects: The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.
Implement the subtraction operation on XPath objects: The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.
Implement the multiply operation on XPath objects: The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.
Implement the div operation on XPath objects arg1 / arg2.

detaileddescription.para: The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.

briefdescription.para: Implement the mod operation on XPath objects: arg1 / arg2 The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.
Is the name given a NodeType one.

detaileddescription.para: [38] NodeType ::= 'comment' | 'text' | 'processing-instruction' | 'node'

simplesect.para: 1 if true 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "self" direction The self axis contains just the context node itself.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "child" direction The child axis contains the children of the context node in document order.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "descendant" direction the descendant axis contains the descendants of the context node in document order; a descendant is a child or a child of a child and so on.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "descendant-or-self" direction the descendant-or-self axis contains the context node and the descendants of the context node in document order; thus the context node is the first node on the axis, and the first child of the context node is the second node on the axis.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "parent" direction The parent axis contains the parent of the context node, if there is one.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "ancestor-or-self" direction he ancestor-or-self axis contains the context node and ancestors of the context node in reverse document order; thus the context node is the first node on the axis, and the context node's parent the second; parent here is defined the same as with the parent axis.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "following-sibling" direction The following-sibling axis contains the following siblings of the context node in document order.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "following" direction The following axis contains all nodes in the same document as the context node that are after the context node in document order, excluding any descendants and excluding attribute nodes and namespace nodes; the nodes are ordered in document order.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "namespace" direction the namespace axis contains the namespace nodes of the context node; the order of nodes on this axis is implementation-defined; the axis will be empty unless the context node is an element.

detaileddescription.para: We keep the XML namespace node at the end of the list.

parameterdescription.para: the current attribute in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "attribute" direction TODO: support DTD inherited default attributes.

parameterdescription.para: the current attribute in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "preceding" direction the preceding axis contains all nodes in the same document as the context node that are before the context node in document order, excluding any ancestors and excluding attribute nodes and namespace nodes; the nodes are ordered in reverse document order.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "ancestor" direction the ancestor axis contains the ancestors of the context node; the ancestors of the context node consist of the parent of context node and the parent's parent and so on; the nodes are ordered in reverse document order; thus the parent is the first node on the axis, and the parent's parent is the second node on the axis.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "preceding-sibling" direction The preceding-sibling axis contains the preceding siblings of the context node in reverse document order; the first preceding sibling is first on the axis; the sibling preceding that node is the second on the axis and so on.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Implement the last() XPath function number last() The last function returns the number of nodes in the context node list.
Implement the position() XPath function number position() The position function returns the position of the context node in the context node list.

detaileddescription.para: The first position is 1, and so the last position will be equal to last().

briefdescription.para: Implement the count() XPath function number count(node-set)
Implement the id() XPath function node-set id(object) The id function selects elements by their unique ID (see [5.2.1 Unique IDs]).

detaileddescription.para: When the argument to id is of type node-set, then the result is the union of the result of applying id to the string value of each of the nodes in the argument node-set. When the argument to id is of any other type, the argument is converted to a string as if by a call to the string function; the string is split into a whitespace-separated list of tokens (whitespace is any sequence of characters matching the production S); the result is a node-set containing the elements in the same document as the context node that have a unique ID equal to any of the tokens in the list.

briefdescription.para: Implement the local-name() XPath function string local-name(node-set?) The local-name function returns a string containing the local part of the name of the node in the argument node-set that is first in document order.

detaileddescription.para: If the node-set is empty or the first node has no name, an empty string is returned. If the argument is omitted it defaults to the context node.

briefdescription.para: Implement the namespace-uri() XPath function string namespace-uri(node-set?) The namespace-uri function returns a string containing the namespace URI of the expanded name of the node in the argument node-set that is first in document order.

detaileddescription.para: If the node-set is empty, the first node has no name, or the expanded name has no namespace URI, an empty string is returned. If the argument is omitted it defaults to the context node.

briefdescription.para: Implement the string() XPath function string string(object?) The string function converts an object to a string as follows:

listitem.para: A node-set is converted to a string by returning the value of the node in the node-set that is first in document order. If the node-set is empty, an empty string is returned.

A number is converted to a string as follows
NaN is converted to the string NaN
positive zero is converted to the string 0
negative zero is converted to the string 0
positive infinity is converted to the string Infinity
negative infinity is converted to the string -Infinity
if the number is an integer, the number is represented in decimal form as a Number with no decimal point and no leading zeros, preceded by a minus sign (-) if the number is negative
otherwise, the number is represented in decimal form as a Number including a decimal point with at least one digit before the decimal point and at least one digit after the decimal point, preceded by a minus sign (-) if the number is negative; there must be no leading zeros before the decimal point apart possibly from the one required digit immediately before the decimal point; beyond the one required digit after the decimal point there must be as many, but only as many, more digits as are needed to uniquely distinguish the number from all other IEEE 754 numeric values.
The boolean false value is converted to the string false. The boolean true value is converted to the string true.

detaileddescription.para: If the argument is omitted, it defaults to a node-set with the context node as its only member.

briefdescription.para: Implement the string-length() XPath function number string-length(string?) The string-length returns the number of characters in the string (see [3.6 Strings]).

detaileddescription.para: If the argument is omitted, it defaults to the context node converted to a string, in other words the value of the context node.

briefdescription.para: Implement the concat() XPath function string concat(string, string, string*) The concat function returns the concatenation of its arguments.

Implement the contains() XPath function boolean contains(string, string) The contains function returns true if the first argument string contains the second argument string, and otherwise returns false.

Implement the starts-with() XPath function boolean starts-with(string, string) The starts-with function returns true if the first argument string starts with the second argument string, and otherwise returns false.

Implement the substring() XPath function string substring(string, number, number?) The substring function returns the substring of the first argument starting at the position specified in the second argument with length specified in the third argument.

detaileddescription.para:
For example, substring("12345",2,3) returns "234". If the third argument is not specified, it returns the substring starting at the position specified in the second argument and continuing to the end of the string. For example, substring("12345",2) returns "2345". More precisely, each character in the string (see [3.6 Strings]) is considered to have a numeric position: the position of the first character is 1, the position of the second character is 2 and so on. The returned substring contains those characters for which the position of the character is greater than or equal to the second argument and, if the third argument is specified, less than the sum of the second and third arguments; the comparisons and addition used for the above follow the standard IEEE 754 rules. Thus:
substring("12345", 1.5, 2.6) returns "234"
substring("12345", 0, 3) returns "12"
substring("12345", 0 div 0, 3) returns ""
substring("12345", 1, 0 div 0) returns ""
substring("12345", -42, 1 div 0) returns "12345"
substring("12345", -1 div 0, 1 div 0) returns ""

briefdescription.para: Implement the substring-before() XPath function string substring-before(string, string) The substring-before function returns the substring of the first argument string that precedes the first occurrence of the second argument string in the first argument string, or the empty string if the first argument string does not contain the second argument string.

detaileddescription.para: For example, substring-before("1999/04/01","/") returns 1999.

briefdescription.para: Implement the substring-after() XPath function string substring-after(string, string) The substring-after function returns the substring of the first argument string that follows the first occurrence of the second argument string in the first argument string, or the empty string if the first argument string does not contain the second argument string.

detaileddescription.para: For example, substring-after("1999/04/01","/") returns 04/01, and substring-after("1999/04/01","19") returns 99/04/01.

briefdescription.para: Implement the normalize-space() XPath function string normalize-space(string?) The normalize-space function returns the argument string with white space normalized by stripping leading and trailing whitespace and replacing sequences of whitespace characters by a single space.

detaileddescription.para: Whitespace characters are the same allowed by the S production in XML. If the argument is omitted, it defaults to the context node converted to a string, in other words the value of the context node.

briefdescription.para: Implement the translate() XPath function string translate(string, string, string) The translate function returns the first argument string with occurrences of characters in the second argument string replaced by the character at the corresponding position in the third argument string.

detaileddescription.para: For example, translate("bar","abc","ABC") returns the string BAr. If there is a character in the second argument string with no character at a corresponding position in the third argument string (because the second argument string is longer than the third argument string), then occurrences of that character in the first argument string are removed. For example, translate("--aaa--","abc-","ABC") returns "AAA". If a character occurs more than once in second argument string, then the first occurrence determines the replacement character. If the third argument string is longer than the second argument string, then excess characters are ignored.

briefdescription.para: Implement the not() XPath function boolean not(boolean) The not function returns true if its argument is false, and false otherwise.
Implement the true() XPath function boolean true()
Implement the false() XPath function boolean false()

Implement the lang() XPath function boolean lang(string) The lang function returns true or false depending on whether the language of the context node as specified by xml:lang attributes is the same as or is a sublanguage of the language specified by the argument string.

detaileddescription.para: The language of the context node is determined by the value of the xml:lang attribute on the context node, or, if the context node has no xml:lang attribute, by the value of the xml:lang attribute on the nearest ancestor of the context node that has an xml:lang attribute. If there is no such attribute, then lang returns false. If there is such an attribute, then lang returns true if the attribute value is equal to the argument ignoring case, or if there is some suffix starting with - such that the attribute value is equal to the argument ignoring that suffix of the attribute value and ignoring case.

briefdescription.para: Implement the number() XPath function number number(object?)
Implement the sum() XPath function number sum(node-set) The sum function returns the sum of the values of the nodes in the argument node-set.
Implement the floor() XPath function number floor(number) The floor function returns the largest (closest to positive infinity) number that is not greater than the argument and that is an integer.

Implement the ceiling() XPath function number ceiling(number) The ceiling function returns the smallest (closest to negative infinity) number that is not less than the argument and that is an integer.
Implement the round() XPath function number round(number) The round function returns the number that is closest to the argument and that is an integer.

detaileddescription.para: If there are two such numbers, then the one that is closest to positive infinity is returned.

briefdescription.para: Implement the boolean() XPath function boolean boolean(object) The boolean function converts its argument to a boolean as follows:

listitem.para: a number is true if and only if it is neither positive or negative zero nor NaN
a node-set is true if and only if it is non-empty
a string is true if and only if its length is non-zero

briefdescription.para: Really internal functions.

detaileddescription.para: Really internal functions.
In a node set the namespace nodes are duplicated and the next pointer is set to the parent node in the XPath semantic. Check if such a node needs to be freed

parameterdescription.para: the XPath namespace node found in a nodeset.

briefdescription.para: internal interfaces for XML Path Language implementation

detaileddescription.para: internal interfaces for XML Path Language implementation used to build new modules on top of XPath like XPointer and XSLT

simplesect.para: See Copyright for the status of this software.
Daniel Veillard
